page: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance is built on the Nitro hypervisor and utilizes the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by bottom-25% scores in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core capabilities are average, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores falling into the middle 50%. Notably, memory bandwidth for large blocks, both read and write, is strong, placing in the top 25%. PassMark CPU extended instructions also demonstrate strong performance.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is the contrast between its weak single-threaded execution and its strong memory bandwidth and high memory capacity. The local NVMe SSD storage and high memory density make it suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and multi-threaded processing. It is configured for workloads such as database hosting, caching layers, and large-scale data processing where RAM capacity and local storage speed are prioritized over single-core compute performance.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 32 physical Intel Xeon 8259CL cores at 3.1 GHz, and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, falling into the bottom 25%, whereas large-block memory read and write bandwidths are strong, ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and web serving throughput remain in the average tier. This hardware profile, combining high memory density with fast local NVMe storage, is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity but do not depend on high single-thread compute speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong large-block memory bandwidth alongside average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2400 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized configuration featuring high-capacity DDR4 RAM, local NVMe SSD storage, and strong large-block memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong large-block read and write bandwidth
2400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for memory-intensive databases and multi-threaded data processing
